title: source_control_org_policy
description: Org/group-level governance settings for a source-code hosting platform — a GitHub organization, GitLab group, or Bitbucket workspace. Singleton: each org/group emits exactly one record. The single cross-vendor shape policies use for org-wide MFA enforcement, default member access, and secure-by-default repository creation. Plugins normalize platform-specific org settings into these fields; collecting them requires an org-admin-scoped credential.

id.description: Org/group identifier — GitHub org login, GitLab group path, Bitbucket workspace slug. Stable per org.

provider.description: Platform short name: github, gitlab, bitbucket.

two_factor_required.description: Org enforces two-factor authentication for every member (org-wide requirement, not merely observed per user).

default_member_repository_permission.description: Baseline permission every member gets on org repositories. `none`/`read` are least-privilege; `write`/`admin` grant broad default access.

members_can_create_public_repos.description: Members (non-admins) may create public repositories. Optional — omitted by platforms with no such setting.

web_commit_signoff_required.description: Org requires sign-off on web-based commits. Optional.

advanced_security_enabled_new_repos.description: Advanced Security (code/secret scanning suite) is enabled by default on newly created repos. Optional — license-gated on GitHub; omitted where unavailable.

secret_scanning_enabled_new_repos.description: Secret scanning is enabled by default on newly created repos. Optional.

dependabot_alerts_enabled_new_repos.description: Dependency vulnerability alerts are enabled by default on newly created repos. Optional.
